parallel_group.prompt: Create the kbu-run skill bundle for the kbu-harness PRD (KBUtilLib/agent-io/prds/kbu-harness/fullprompt.md, Module 2 + the 'Confront round 1/2 folded resolutions' blocks + Acceptance Criteria #25, #26, #37). Add `src/kbutillib/harness/skills/kbu-run/SKILL.md` (create the dirs). Frontmatter must be valid Claude Code: `name: kbu-run`; a `description` starting with 'Use when ...' scoped to running a local COBRA/MSModelUtil modeling project pulled from a BERIL clone into a kbu harness; `allowed-tools` (Read, Bash); `user-invocable: true`. The body must encode the harness workflow loop: (1) `kbu harness pull` (the design-deploy step); (2) classify the run by the skill's JUDGMENT applying the PRD-A graduated-execution policy (parse only the fenced ```yaml``` block in the harness `.claude/kbu/preferences.md` for `execution.runtime_threshold_seconds` and `execution.fanout_threshold`; default to the full/consult class when uncertain or the block is absent); (3) choose local vs h100 (no encoded default; for h100 dispatch via `kbu harness run --on h100`); (4) `kbu harness run`; (5) verify each notebook executed with outputs from the RunResult; (6) append a DEVLOG.md entry; (7) on SUCCESS: stop and emit the single-line prompt `Push results back to BERIL now? (y/N)`, push (`kbu harness push`) only on y/Y, then remind the user to commit in BERIL (commit-with-outputs rule); on FAILURE: stop, append the nbconvert stderr (<=10k bytes) as an inline `traceback: |` block in DEVLOG.md, escalate a BLOCKED report, and edit NO code. The skill must NOT call the Anthropic API or subprocess `claude` (the worker IS Claude). Add `tests/harness/test_skill_bundle.py` asserting the SKILL.md frontmatter parses and contains name=kbu-run, a 'Use when' description, allowed-tools, and user-invocable:true. Do not implement the CLI here (that is Phase 2); reference the documented `kbu harness` verbs only.
parallel_group.success_criteria: `src/kbutillib/harness/skills/kbu-run/SKILL.md` exists with valid Claude Code frontmatter (name=kbu-run, a 'Use when ...' description, allowed-tools, user-invocable:true) and documents the pull->classify->run->verify->devlog->(success:confirm-push / failure:report-no-edit) loop; `pytest tests/harness/test_skill_bundle.py` passes asserting the frontmatter parses with those fields; no Anthropic API / subprocess claude usage.

parallel_group.prompt: Implement the kbu-harness library + CLI per KBUtilLib/agent-io/prds/kbu-harness/fullprompt.md (Module 1, both 'Confront round 1/2 folded resolutions' blocks, and Acceptance Criteria #1-#24, #27-#37). Build the package `src/kbutillib/harness/` with internal modules: `config.py` (harness.toml load/save; fields beril_root+harness_root absolute, project_id, created_at ISO-8601 UTC Z, kbutillib_version [dist version else source_commit HEAD sha], optional python abs interpreter path; upward-search for nearest harness.toml from CWD), `scaffold.py` (init: validate BERIL root by PROJECT.md + .claude/skills/ present [warn-not-fail on missing .git] and projects/<id>/ exists; sanitize project-id to [a-z0-9._-]; default harness root ~/Dropbox/Projects/kbu-harness/ created-if-absent, --harness-root abs-or-relative, refuse non-empty target unless --force; git init no-autocommit; mirror dirs notebooks/data/user_data/figures; .gitignore [.venv/ __pycache__/ *.egg-info/ .ipynb_checkpoints/ .DS_Store **/.kbcache/]; empty DEVLOG.md; copy the kbu-run skill bundle from src/kbutillib/harness/skills/ into <harness>/.claude/skills/; copy BERIL .claude/kbu/preferences.md or render the bundled template at src/kbutillib/beril/skills/kbu/preferences.md; build a py3.11 venv reusing the kbu-bootstrap venv/_template_ops helpers [venvman path then plain venv fallback], pip -U pip wheel, pip install kbutillib UNLESS source checkout [Path(kbutillib.__file__).parents[1].name=='src' and pyproject two levels up], then -r requirements.txt if present; record python path; then initial pull), `sync.py` (pull/push via `rsync -aH --delete --info=stats2` with excludes .git/ .venv/ __pycache__/ .ipynb_checkpoints/ .DS_Store, trailing slash on the project dir both sides to contain --delete, .kbcache/ included [--exclude-kbcache opt-in to skip], symlinks preserved no -L, print exact command, shutil.which('rsync') probe else `✗ rsync not found on PATH` exit 1, --dry-run -> rsync --dry-run --itemize-changes copies-nothing exit-0; pull refuses on non-empty `git status --porcelain` unless --force; push refuses when BERIL->harness `rsync --dry-run --itemize-changes` shows incoming changes unless --force; preferences one-way BERIL->harness only, newer = mtime > harness copy by >=1s, never overwrite local edits unless --force, never push prefs back), `runner.py` (run: default --on local, discover sorted(notebooks/*.ipynb) excluding dot/.ipynb_checkpoints lexicographic, stop at first failure, no-match -> `✗ no notebooks matched in notebooks/` exit 2; execute via the harness venv interpreter `python -m jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute --inplace --ExecutePreprocessor.kernel_name=python3 <nb>`; never edit source [outputs-in-place is intended]; RunResult {notebook,executed,exit_code,error,outputs_present,runtime_s} where error=nbconvert stderr trimmed to 10k bytes and outputs_present counts code-cell stream/execute_result-with-data/display_data outputs ignoring error outputs [nbformat>=5]; --json prints {results:[...],overall_status:ok|partial|failed}; run never auto-pushes; --on h100 writes a UTF-8 file kbu-<project-id>-<timestamp>.task.md to the resolved h100 inbox [--h100-inbox PATH then KBU_H100_INBOX then default ~/Dropbox/Projects/AIAssistant/cowork-inbox/h100/] containing a fenced shell block `cd '<abs harness path>'; kbu harness run --on local <notebooks>` [single-quoted notebooks, NO git commit, no auto-push] and does no local execution; absent inbox -> `✗ h100 inbox not found at <path>` exit 1 no-crash; all subprocess calls pass args as lists, no shell=True), `devlog.py` (append-only DEVLOG.md; entry `## <ISO-8601 UTC Z> — <pull|run|push>` + fenced yaml block notebooks/scope/where/outcome/runtime_s/optional traceback[<=10k inline]; never rewrite existing entries; no file locking). Doctor: run under the harness venv interpreter [✗+nonzero if absent], check venv present, import kbutillib, harness.toml valid + beril_root exists, python path present+existent, nbconvert importable; CRAFT-style ✓/✗ lines + fixed summary `kbu harness doctor summary:` / `  Checks OK: X/Y` / `  Checks FAIL: Z`, exit 0 iff Z==0. Add `src/kbutillib/cli/harness.py` exposing init/pull/push/run/doctor (and optional status) and register it in `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` via `main.add_command(harness_cmd, name='harness')`, matching the existing kbu beril output helpers (`── ` step prefix, `✓ `/`✗ ` lines, `Summary:` block, errors `✗ `-prefixed); exit codes pull/push 0 ok /1 partial /2 source-missing, run 0 all-ok /1 some-failed /2 none-matched, doctor 0/1. Add tests under tests/harness/: library core (init scaffold against a fake BERIL root incl. the kbu-run bundle landing + harness.toml round-trip + --force/non-empty-refusal), pull/push round-trip both ways with .kbcache included + excludes honored + --dry-run copies-nothing, runner on a generated clean notebook [executed+outputs_present true] and a generated throwing notebook [failure RunResult, source unchanged], --on h100 writing a well-formed task file to a TEMP inbox override [never the real Dropbox path] with no execution, devlog append-only, and a doctor matrix [missing venv, import-fail, malformed harness.toml, missing beril_root, missing nbconvert]; generate notebooks on the fly via nbformat.v4.new_notebook(), no binary .ipynb committed, all tests in temp dirs. Do NOT delete or modify the old co-scientist surface (kbu-start/plan/build/migrate, kbu-sub-*, cli/notebook.py, cli/subproject.py, cli/manifest.py, layout.py) — that strip is a deferred separate PRD. Keep the existing KBUtilLib test suite green (0 regressions).
parallel_group.success_criteria: `kbu harness {init,pull,push,run,doctor}` is implemented and registered on the kbu CLI per the PRD Acceptance Criteria; `pytest tests/harness/` passes covering library-core (init scaffold against a fake BERIL root including the kbu-run bundle landing, harness.toml round-trip, --force/refusal), pull/push round-trip both ways with .kbcache included and excludes honored, --dry-run copies nothing, runner on generated clean+throwing notebooks (failure RunResult with source unchanged), --on h100 writing a well-formed task file to a temp inbox override with no execution, devlog append-only, and the doctor matrix; rsync uses -aH and is probed via shutil.which; the old co-scientist surface is untouched; the full existing KBUtilLib test suite passes with 0 regressions.
